findings.evidence: `JHolomorphic.lean:80-87` has `HasFDerivAt` inside `IsJHolomorphicAt` and `IsJHolomorphicOn := ∀ x ∈ s, IsJHolomorphicAt ...`; Mathlib defines `DifferentiableOn` as `∀ x ∈ s, DifferentiableWithinAt 𝕜 f s x` in `Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/FDeriv/Defs.lean:163`, with `HasFDerivWithinAt` at lines 115-116.
findings.fix: Define a within-set variant using `HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x` and the same complex-linearity condition, make `IsJHolomorphicOn` use it, and provide `fderivWithin` commutation APIs; keep the ambient version only as an `At`/open-set specialization.
findings.issue: `IsJHolomorphicOn` expands to `∀ x ∈ s, IsJHolomorphicAt ... x`, so it requires an ambient `HasFDerivAt` at every point of `s`; for non-open domains this is stronger than differentiability/holomorphicity on the domain and will reject boundary-domain uses expected in the roadmap.

summary: The pointwise `IsJHolomorphicAt` predicate matches the derivative-level Cauchy-Riemann equation, but the setwise predicate formalizes the wrong notion for maps on a subset/domain.
